title: Understanding the Density Function
text: The density of the rod is given by the function $$rho(x)=\rho_0+\left(\rho_1-\rho_0\right) \left(\frac{x}{L}\right)^2$$. This means the density changes quadratically from one end of the rod to the other.

title: Formula for Center of Mass
text: The center of mass for a continuous object is given by the formula x_cm=(1/M)*int{x*rho(x),0,L,x}, where M is the total mass of the rod.

title: Finding the Total Mass
text: To find the total mass M of the rod, integrate the density function over the length of the rod: $$M=int{rho(x), 0, L, x$$.}

title: Calculate the Total Mass
text: Calculate the total mass M of the rod using the integral M=int{rho_0+(rho_1-rho_0)*(x/L)**2,0,L,x}.

title: Substitute the Density Function
text: Substitute the density function $$rho(x)=\rho_0+\left(\rho_1-\rho_0\right) \left(\frac{x}{L}\right)^2$$ into the center of mass formula.

title: Calculate the Center of Mass
text: Calculate the center of mass $$x_{cm}$$ using the formula x_cm=(1/M)*int{x*(rho_0+(rho_1-rho_0)*(x/L)**2),0,L,x}.
